evals.prompt: Enhance the README for this Node.js CLI tool project. The repo is at github.com/acme/taskrunner, it has a package.json with name 'taskrunner' version '2.1.0', a LICENSE file with MIT, a .github/workflows/ci.yml, and the primary language is TypeScript.
evals.expected_output: README.md updated with shields.io badges (version, build, license, language, stars, downloads, PRs welcome), an SVG infographic saved to .github/readme-infographic.svg with 1280x640 viewBox, and a collapsible prompt details section in the README.
expectations: README.md contains at least one badge URL matching 'https://img.shields.io/' with 'style=for-the-badge' query parameter
README.md contains an npm version badge with 'npm/v/taskrunner' in the URL
README.md contains a license badge referencing MIT
An SVG file is written to .github/readme-infographic.svg containing a root <svg> element with viewBox='0 0 1280 640'
README.md contains a <details> block with <summary> referencing 'infographic generation prompt' or similar wording

README.md contains an tag referencing '.github/readme-infographic.svg' wrapped in a center-aligned

element

evals.prompt: Enhance the README for my Python library 'dataweave' hosted at github.com/jsmith/dataweave. It has a pyproject.toml with version 0.8.3, an Apache-2.0 LICENSE, GitHub Actions CI, and Codecov integration.
evals.expected_output: README.md updated with Python-specific badges (PyPI version, build status, coverage, license, language), an SVG infographic using the Library/SDK balanced layout, and the collapsible prompt section.
expectations: README.md contains a PyPI version badge with 'pypi/v/dataweave' in the URL and 'logo=python' parameter
README.md contains a Codecov coverage badge with 'codecov/c/github/jsmith/dataweave' in the URL
README.md contains a license badge referencing Apache 2.0 or Apache_2.0
The SVG infographic contains a linearGradient element for the background fill
README.md badge lines appear after the first H1 heading and before the infographic image element
The SVG infographic contains text elements with the project name 'dataweave'

evals.prompt: Enhance the README for github.com/acme/taskrunner with --badges-only mode. It has package.json (name: taskrunner, version: 3.0.0), MIT LICENSE, and GitHub Actions CI.
evals.expected_output: README.md updated with badges only; no infographic SVG file is generated and no infographic image tag is inserted into the README.
expectations: README.md contains shields.io badge markdown with 'style=for-the-badge'
No SVG file is created at .github/readme-infographic.svg
README.md does not contain an tag referencing 'readme-infographic'
README.md contains a build status badge referencing 'github/actions/workflow/status'
Badges are ordered with status/version badges appearing before engagement badges (stars, PRs welcome)

evals.prompt: Enhance the README for github.com/acme/taskrunner with --infographic-only mode. It has package.json (name: taskrunner, version: 1.5.0), MIT LICENSE, and TypeScript source files.
evals.expected_output: An SVG infographic is generated and placed in the README, but existing badges are not modified or added.
expectations: An SVG file is written to .github/readme-infographic.svg with viewBox='0 0 1280 640'
README.md contains an tag or element referencing the infographic SVG
README.md contains a <details> block with the collapsible generation prompt section
No new shields.io badge lines are added to README.md beyond any that already existed
The SVG contains a footer section with an install command or project link text

evals.prompt: Enhance the README for a project at github.com/devuser/myutil that has no package.json, no Cargo.toml, no pyproject.toml, and no go.mod. It only has a README.md, some .sh scripts, and a LICENSE (MIT).
evals.expected_output: The skill extracts the project name from the git remote URL, generates badges using GitHub-level information (license, stars, last commit), and creates an infographic using available context.
expectations: README.md contains a license badge for MIT even without a package manifest
README.md contains GitHub engagement badges (stars or last-commit) using 'devuser/myutil' in the URL
No registry-specific version badge (npm, PyPI, crates.io) is present since there is no package manifest
An SVG infographic is still generated at .github/readme-infographic.svg with valid SVG markup
The SVG infographic has a 1280x640 viewBox and contains the project name 'myutil' in a text element

evals.prompt: Enhance the README for github.com/acme/nightvision. It has package.json (name: nightvision, version: 1.0.0), MIT LICENSE, and a .claude/github-social.local.md config with dark_mode set to 'both'.
evals.expected_output: README.md uses a <picture> element with <source> tags for dark and light mode infographic variants, and both SVG files are generated.
expectations: README.md contains a <picture> element with a <source> tag having media='(prefers-color-scheme: dark)' and srcset referencing a dark variant SVG
README.md contains a <source> tag with media='(prefers-color-scheme: light)' referencing the light variant SVG
Two SVG files are generated: the default infographic and a dark mode variant (e.g., readme-infographic-dark.svg)
The element contains a fallback tag referencing the default infographic SVG

evals.prompt: Enhance the README for github.com/bigcorp/megaplatform. It has package.json (name: megaplatform, version: 5.2.1), MIT LICENSE, GitHub Actions CI, Codecov, a Discord community link in README, a FUNDING.yml, docs at docs.megaplatform.io, and TypeScript as primary language. Ensure badges follow proper ordering and stay within limits.
evals.expected_output: README.md contains a comprehensive but bounded badge set (8-12 badges) ordered by importance: status first, then version, quality, language, metrics, engagement, community, and support last.
expectations: README.md contains between 8 and 12 shields.io badge lines (not exceeding 15)
The build status badge appears before the npm version badge in the README source order
The version badge appears before stars/downloads engagement badges in source order
README.md contains a Discord badge with 'logo=discord' in the URL
README.md contains a sponsor or funding-related badge since FUNDING.yml exists
README.md contains a documentation badge referencing docs.megaplatform.io
